items.front: What is the core rule for issuing an instrument approach clearance?
items.back: Clear only a published standard or special approach, using its published name. If the route to the approach is unpublished, issue an altitude to maintain until the aircraft is established.

items.front: What extra wording is required when clearing an approach to an uncontrolled airport?
items.back: State the name of the airport in the approach clearance.

items.front: What must be included when an approach clearance is relayed through a non-air-carrier communication station?
items.back: Include the required weather report, or instruct the station to issue current weather.

items.front: When may altitudes above the high-altitude military approach chart be assigned?
items.back: They may be assigned when required for separation, using altitudes coordinated with the military authority that originated the procedure.

items.front: When must approach-clearance altitude be stated?
items.back: State the charted altitude when adherence to it is required for separation. You may omit it when separation will be ensured by the pilot following the published maximum, minimum, or mandatory altitudes.

items.front: What are the main limits on issuing a circling approach?
items.back: Only issue circling instructions at airports with operational towers, specify the runway and direction when required, and do not issue instructions that would take the aircraft outside the protected circling area.

items.front: When may a side-step maneuver be authorized?
items.back: Only when the instrument approach procedure authorizes it, allowing approach to one runway and landing on the parallel runway.

items.front: When may an IFR aircraft be released to advisory frequency?
items.back: When it intends to land at an airport without a tower or FSS and you no longer require direct communications.

items.front: What does an approach clearance automatically authorize regarding a missed approach?
items.back: Except for VFR practice approaches, an approach clearance automatically authorizes the published missed approach. If another missed approach is needed, issue the published alternate or one already published by NOTAM.

items.front: What extra information is given when a pilot says they are unfamiliar with the approach?
items.back: Specify the initial approach altitude, procedure-turn direction and distance, procedure-turn altitude, final course and altitude, and any needed missed-approach information or chart notations.

items.front: How are practice instrument approaches handled for IFR and VFR aircraft?
items.back: Approve practice approaches only when workload permits. IFR practice approaches get IFR separation. VFR practice approaches require maintain-VFR handling and get IFR separation only where directives require it.

items.front: How is an aircraft treated after a touch-and-go, low approach, or practice approach?
items.back: Treat it as arriving until touchdown or threshold crossing, then as departing. Before final descent, issue the climb-out or departure instructions to be followed afterward.
